title: Scirpus pterolepis
intro: Scirpus pterolepis, also known as Marsh Club-rush or Marsh Club-sedge, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Scirpus pterolepis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
